auth.enterEmailFirst: Enter your email above first, then click reset.
auth.signInSubtitle: Use your work email and password.
auth.footer: Account requests go through your administrator.

errors.tooManyRequests: Too many attempts. Try again later or reset your password.

posts.title: Posts
posts.noPosts: No posts yet. Create your first one to get started.
posts.noPostsInFilter: No posts match the current filter.

bulk.confirmDelete_one: Delete {{count}} post? This unpublishes from Flexweg and cannot be undone.
bulk.confirmDelete_other: Delete {{count}} posts? This unpublishes from Flexweg and cannot be undone.

slugCollision.post: This URL is already used by post: "{{label}}".
slugCollision.page: This URL is already used by page: "{{label}}".
slugCollision.category: This URL is already used by category: "{{label}}".

inspector.blockEmpty: Select a block in the editor to see its options.

blocks.noSelection: Select a block in the editor to see its options.

pages.title: Pages
pages.homeBadgeTooltip: Used as the site's home page

taxonomies.title: Categories & Tags
taxonomies.slugCollision: This slug is already used by "{{label}}".

regenerate.allHtmlHelp: Every post, page, archive and listing — plus menu / posts / authors JSON snapshots.
regenerate.assetsHelp: Pushes the active theme's CSS + companion scripts to /theme-assets/.
regenerate.everythingHelp: Theme assets + all HTML + every plugin output. Slowest option.
regenerate.noPluginTargets: No plugin regeneration targets — enable a plugin that supports regenerate.

confirmSwitch.title: Switch to {{theme}}?
confirmSwitch.body: Switching theme requires re-uploading the theme's assets and regenerating every published page so the new look propagates everywhere. This may take a moment.

site.title: Site
site.siteLanguageHelp: BCP-47 tag (e.g. en, fr-CA) injected into <html lang="…"> on every published page. The prefix is matched against supported translation locales for sitemap/RSS labels and theme-baked strings; unsupported locales fall back to English.

flexweg.title: Flexweg API
flexweg.apiKeyHelp: Generated from your Flexweg account → API. Stored in Firestore.
flexweg.siteUrlHelp: Public URL of your Flexweg site without trailing slash.

performance.title: Performance
performance.help: How the admin loads posts and pages. Default works on the free Firestore plan with no setup; the paginated mode is for very large sites and requires a one-time index setup.
performance.globalHelp: Loads the whole posts collection in one live subscription. Sorting, filtering and search happen in memory. Best for sites under ~5 000 posts.
performance.paginatedHelp: Cursor-paginated subscriptions, one page at a time. Requires composite Firestore indexes — see README. Recommended for very large sites.
performance.savedReload: Saved. Reload the admin to apply the new mode.

editor.title: Editor
editor.help: Typography used inside the post / page editor. Independent of the public theme.
editor.sizeHelp: CSS length, e.g. 16px or 1rem. Leave empty for default.

profile.title: Profile
profile.adminLocaleHelp: Language used by the admin interface. Saved to your profile.
profile.headline: Title
profile.headlineHelp: Shown publicly under your name in author cards and on your archive page.
profile.bioPlaceholder: A short paragraph shown on your author page and below your articles.
profile.bioHelp: Visible on the public site in the author bio sidebar and the author archive page.
profile.socialsHelp: Add the URLs of the profiles you want to surface on the public site, then tick "Show" for the ones you want visible. Empty URLs are ignored.
profile.socialsShowHelp: Toggle the visibility of this network on the public site.
profile.avatarHelp: Pick an image from your media library. Square images render best.

firestoreSetup.title: Firestore setup needed
firestoreSetup.intro: The post and page list pages need a couple of Firestore composite indexes to query efficiently. They have to be created manually — Firebase reserves index creation for project admins.
firestoreSetup.noUrl: Couldn't extract the auto-suggest URL — see the README for the gcloud command.
firestoreSetup.step1: Click the link for each missing index — Firebase Console opens with the index definition pre-filled.
firestoreSetup.step2: Click "Create" and wait 1–5 minutes for the index to build (status badge in the console).
firestoreSetup.step3: Come back here and click Retry. Once both queries succeed, the admin loads.
firestoreSetup.buildHint: Indexes can take up to a few minutes to build after creation.
firestoreSetup.unexpectedHint: Check your network connection and Firestore rules. If you just created the indexes, wait a moment for them to finish building, then retry.

errors.loadAccountHint: If the problem persists, sign out and try again.
errors.accountDisabledMessage: Your access to this CMS has been disabled. Contact your administrator.
errors.adminEmailMessage: VITE_ADMIN_EMAIL is missing in .env. Set it to the email of the bootstrap administrator.
errors.consoleHint: Check the browser console and your .env configuration. See the README for setup instructions.

setup.title: Welcome to Flexweg CMS
setup.subtitle: First-time setup
setup.intro: Fill in your Firebase credentials, the bootstrap administrator account, and your Flexweg API key. The values are validated, written to your project (Firestore + a config.js file on Flexweg), and the admin reloads into the normal sign-in flow. You only do this once.

welcome.heading: Welcome to Flexweg CMS!
welcome.intro: Flexweg CMS is a free tool offered by Flexweg to publish pages on your Flexweg hosting — no extra fees from Flexweg, ever. To run, it needs a free Firebase project (Auth + Firestore) — that's where it stores your posts, media metadata and settings.
welcome.firebaseBody: Creating a Firebase project is free and takes about 5 minutes: open the Firebase Console, create a new project, enable Authentication (email/password) and Firestore. The Spark plan is enough for the typical site. Our tutorial walks you through every click.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.intro: Please read and accept the terms below before using Flexweg CMS. They are written in plain language to be quick to read.

section1.title: 1. Free service
section1.body: Flexweg CMS is provided to you free of charge by Flexweg. You can use it without limits with your Flexweg hosting — Flexweg will never bill you for using the CMS itself. Standard charges for your Flexweg hosting plan and your Firebase plan still apply, on the respective accounts you own.

section2.title: 2. Provided as-is, no warranty
section2.body: The CMS is provided as-is. We work to keep it stable and improve it over time, but we cannot guarantee it will be free of bugs, defects or interruptions. Use it with the same caution you would any tool you didn't write yourself: keep backups of important content.

section3.title: 3. Your content, your responsibility
section3.body: You are solely responsible for the content you publish through the CMS — text, images, videos, forms, code embeds, and any other media. You must comply with applicable law in your jurisdiction (and in the jurisdictions you target), copyright, image and personality rights, personal data protection rules, and any third-party terms (e.g. embedded fonts or videos). Flexweg is not responsible for content published by users of the CMS.

section4.title: 4. Your Firebase account, your security
section4.body: The CMS stores its data in your own Firebase project. You are responsible for securing that project: choose a strong password for the bootstrap admin account, set up Firestore security rules as documented, rotate your API keys when needed, and review who has access. Flexweg cannot be held responsible for unauthorised access, data leaks, hacks or losses caused by misconfiguration or compromise of your Firebase account.

section5.title: 5. Limitation of liability
section5.body: Flexweg cannot be held responsible for any loss of revenue, loss of data, loss of business opportunity, or any direct or indirect damages arising from the use, misuse, or unavailability of the CMS. Where applicable law requires Flexweg to assume some liability for the CMS, that liability is limited to the amount actually paid to Flexweg for the CMS over the past 12 months — which is zero, since the CMS is free.

section6.title: 6. Your data stays yours
section6.body: Content you create with the CMS is stored in your own Firebase project (text, settings, media metadata) and on your own Flexweg hosting (the published HTML, images, theme assets). The CMS itself does not transmit your content to any third-party service. Plugins or themes you choose to enable may have their own behaviour — review what they do before activating them.

section7.title: 7. Fair use
section7.body: Use of the CMS for unlawful, fraudulent, or harmful activities, or in ways that breach Flexweg's general terms of use, may result in suspension of your Flexweg hosting access. We may also update these CMS terms over time; significant changes will be communicated through the admin or via email.

terms.accept: I have read and accept the Terms of use.

help.firebase: Find these values in Firebase Console → Project settings → General → Your apps → SDK setup and configuration.
help.admin: Use an existing user from Firebase Authentication. Anyone signing in with this email is treated as admin and bypasses the user-record check. Create the user via Firebase Console → Authentication if it doesn't exist yet.
help.flexweg: Generate a permanent API key in your Flexweg account → API. The site URL is the public address of your Flexweg site (e.g. https://your-site.flexweg.com).

setup.reloadingHint: Reloading the admin in a moment — sit tight.
setup.footer: These values are saved on your project (Firestore + Flexweg). They are not sent anywhere else.

errors.firebaseAuthGeneric: Could not sign in to Firebase.
errors.firebaseAuthInvalidCredential: Invalid email or password for the Firebase account.
errors.firebaseAuthUserNotFound: No Firebase user with this email. Create one in Firebase Console → Authentication → Users first.
errors.firebaseAuthInvalidEmail: The admin email is not a valid email address.
errors.firebaseInvalidApiKey: The Firebase API key is invalid. Double-check the value from Firebase Console.
errors.firebaseNetwork: Network error while signing in to Firebase. Check your connection.
errors.firebaseTooManyRequests: Too many sign-in attempts. Try again in a moment or reset the password from Firebase Console.
errors.wrongAdminEmail: The Firebase account you signed in with does not match the admin email you typed. Try again.
errors.flexwegAuth: Flexweg API key rejected. Check the value from your Flexweg account → API.
errors.flexwegNetwork: Network error while contacting Flexweg. Check the API base URL and your connection.
errors.firestoreRulesHint: Open Firebase Console → Firestore → Rules and make sure the bootstrap admin email is set to '{{email}}'. The README has a copy-pastable rules template.
errors.uploadFailed: Could not upload config.js to Flexweg

errors.configMissing: Flexweg API key is not configured. Set it in admin Settings before publishing.
errors.network: {{action}}: network error or CORS blocked. Check your connection.
errors.auth: {{action}}: authentication failed. Check your Flexweg API key in Settings.
errors.tooLarge: {{action}}: file too large for your Flexweg plan.
errors.rateLimited: {{action}}: too many requests, please retry in a moment.
